seoTitle: How to Control Page Numbering by Section in Google Docs
metaDescription: Restart page numbers in specific sections of a Google Doc using section breaks, so your cover page and table of contents stay clean.
intro: Continuous page numbering can ruin the look of a cover page or table of contents in a long report or manuscript. This guide shows how to use section breaks in Google Docs to number different parts of your document independently.

steps.heading: Insert a section break
steps.body: Place your cursor at the end of your intro page, click Insert, hover over Break, and choose 'Section break (next page)'.

steps.heading: Unlink the header or footer
steps.body: Double-click the header or footer on the new section's page, then uncheck 'Link to previous' in the floating toolbar.

steps.heading: Open page number settings
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Page Numbers, and select More Options to open the numbering configuration.

steps.heading: Restart numbering at one
steps.body: Set 'Apply to' to the current section, choose 'Start at', enter 1, and click Apply.

steps.heading: Clean up earlier pages
steps.body: Scroll back to your intro pages, delete any leftover manual page numbers, and confirm the new section numbers correctly going forward.

faq.question: Can I have different numbering formats in different sections?
faq.answer: Yes, once sections are unlinked, each can use its own number format and starting value.

faq.question: Will inserting a section break affect my existing formatting?
faq.answer: It shouldn't, but it's good practice to review margins and headers right after adding a break.

faq.question: How many section breaks can I add to one document?
faq.answer: There's no strict limit, so you can create as many independently numbered sections as your document needs.

faq.question: What if my page numbers still look linked after unchecking the box?
faq.answer: Make sure you unchecked 'Link to previous' on the correct section's header or footer, not the one before it.
